skill.name: zach-listing-health-checker
skill.description: Checks Amazon listing health from a shopper view. It fetches public Amazon pages, extracts listing signals, checks search visibility, and creates a Markdown health report.
skill.summary: Audit Amazon listing visibility, purchase readiness, seller status, delivery, reviews, and search placement.
skill.author: zach22-1999

security_audit.summary: Static analysis correctly identified network access, subprocess execution, and filesystem writes, but most README and SKILL.md hits are documentation false positives. No prompt injection, credential access, weak cryptography, or obfuscated payload evidence was found. Publication is acceptable with a warning because the skill intentionally scrapes Amazon and writes reports to a user-selected path.

medium_findings.title: Outbound Amazon Scraping Through Curl
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The scripts intentionally fetch Amazon product and search pages through curl. This is core functionality, but it sends ASINs and keywords to Amazon and depends on external network behavior.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Network requests are explicit and semantically required by the listing audit workflow. The destination domains are selected from site configuration, so this is intentional rather than hidden exfiltration.

medium_findings.title: User-Controlled Report Output Path
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The command accepts an output directory, creates it, and writes a Markdown report there. This is legitimate reporting behavior, but a user-supplied path can create or overwrite files within the process permissions.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The write path is directly controlled by the --output-dir argument and no allowlist is enforced. The behavior is obvious and useful, so it is a moderate risk rather than malicious.

low_findings.title: Subprocess Use Is Mitigated By Argument List Execution
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE for command injection: curl is executed with subprocess.run using an argument list and no shell=True. The call still creates external process risk, but user input is not interpolated into a shell command.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The subprocess call is visible and uses a fixed executable with list arguments. That strongly reduces shell injection risk, although curl still performs outbound network access.

low_findings.title: Documentation Path Traversal Hits Are Relative Links
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: README relative paths point to documentation and image assets. They are not used by Python filesystem operations and do not create traversal behavior at runtime.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged sequences occur in Markdown links and image references only. I found no evidence that these paths are passed to file APIs.

low_findings.title: Weak Crypto, Credential, IP, And Obfuscation Alerts Lack Evidence
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The flagged lines correspond to front matter, parser code, user-agent text, keywords, or Markdown content. No evidence found for weak cryptographic algorithms, credential files, hardcoded IP endpoints, or obfuscated payloads.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Keyword searches found no cryptographic library use, certificate material, secrets, base64 decoding, eval, or hidden payload logic. The entropy signals are plausibly caused by Chinese text, tables, and Markdown.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Process For HTTP Fetching
dangerous_patterns.description: The shared fetch helper starts curl as a subprocess. This pattern should remain restricted to fixed commands and list arguments.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The external process call is direct and easy to verify. It is mitigated by the absence of shell=True, but still warrants marketplace disclosure.

dangerous_patterns.title: Filesystem Read And Write For Report History
dangerous_patterns.description: The runner reads prior reports and writes a new Markdown report under the selected output directory.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The filesystem operations are explicit and central to report generation. They are limited to report files by pattern and selected output directory, but no path sandbox is enforced.

content.value_statement: Amazon listing problems can hide in price, seller, delivery, review, or search visibility changes. This skill fetches public listing pages and turns those checks into a structured health report.
actual_capabilities: Fetches public Amazon product pages for supported country sites.
Extracts title, price, seller, cart, delivery, category, BSR, rating, review, variant, and stock signals.
Checks keyword search result visibility when keywords are provided.
Compares current results with prior generated reports in the selected output directory.
Generates a Markdown health report with pass, warning, and failure status.
Runs independently through Python scripts and curl after dependencies are installed.
limitations: It only sees public Amazon pages and does not use seller account data.
Amazon anti-bot controls, captchas, and layout changes can reduce extraction quality.
Search results can vary by location, time, and personalization.
It writes local Markdown reports and should be run with a trusted output directory.

use_cases.title: Launch Validation
use_cases.description: Check a new ASIN after launch for page access, Buy Box ownership, purchase buttons, delivery, and first-page search visibility.

use_cases.title: Weekly Listing Patrol
use_cases.description: Run recurring checks for core ASINs and compare reports to spot review, ranking, and visibility changes.

use_cases.title: Sales Drop Triage
use_cases.description: Investigate whether price, seller, cart, stock, delivery, review, or search signals explain a sudden performance change.

prompt_templates.title: Basic ASIN Check
prompt_templates.prompt: Check ASIN B0XXXXXXXX on the US Amazon site and create a listing health report.

prompt_templates.title: Seller Match Check
prompt_templates.scenario: Confirm Buy Box and seller status
prompt_templates.prompt: Check ASIN B0XXXXXXXX on US Amazon. Expected seller is MyBrand. Report seller, cart, price, delivery, and review issues.

prompt_templates.title: Keyword Visibility Check
prompt_templates.prompt: Check ASIN B0XXXXXXXX on US Amazon for seller MyBrand with keywords wireless microphone and karaoke microphone.

prompt_templates.title: Batch Health Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Run a batch health check for B0AAAAAAA1 and B0BBBBBBB2 on US Amazon with seller MyBrand and compare against prior reports.

output_examples.input: ASIN B0XXXXXXXX on US Amazon with seller MyBrand.
output_examples.output: A Markdown report showing overall health, page access, price, seller match, cart buttons, delivery, category, BSR, reviews, and issues.

output_examples.input: ASIN B0XXXXXXXX with keywords wireless microphone and karaoke microphone.
output: Search visibility summary for each keyword.
Position and result type when found.
Warning when the ASIN appears only as sponsored or is absent.

output_examples.input: Two ASINs with a shared output directory containing older reports.
output_examples.output: A batch report with a summary table and history comparison for rankings, reviews, and search position when prior data exists.

best_practices: Use specific ASINs, site code, expected seller name, and two or three core keywords.
Run checks on a consistent schedule so history comparisons are meaningful.
Use a dedicated output directory that contains only trusted listing health reports.
anti_patterns: Do not treat one public-page snapshot as a complete sales diagnosis.
Do not run broad scraping batches without considering Amazon rate limits and policies.
Do not use untrusted output paths or shared directories for report history.

faq.question: Does this skill use Amazon APIs?
faq.answer: No. It fetches public Amazon pages with curl and parses HTML with BeautifulSoup.

faq.question: Does it need seller account credentials?
faq.answer: No. It does not log in and does not request seller account credentials.

faq.question: Which Amazon sites are supported?
faq.answer: It includes US, UK, DE, FR, IT, ES, CA, JP, MX, and AU site configuration.

faq.question: Can it prove why sales declined?
faq.answer: No. It checks visible listing signals that may explain issues, but sales diagnosis needs more data.

faq.question: What happens if Amazon returns a captcha?
faq.answer: The scripts detect captcha-like pages and mark the check as blocked or failed instead of inventing data.

faq.question: Where are reports saved?
faq.answer: The runner writes Markdown reports to the selected output directory, or the current directory by default.
